content.text: Standard Dashboard Templates for Claude Teams

Every team shipping a Claude integration needs the same core observability: are responses fast, are they cheap, are they good? These three questions map to three standard dashboards: Golden Signals, Cost Overview, and Quality Signals. This document provides production-ready Grafana dashboard templates for each, structured as Jsonnet files that plug into the GitOps workflow described in the previous sub-subtopic.

attrs.alt: Standard dashboard suite for Claude teams — three dashboards by audience and content

content.text: Shared Variables and Conventions
All three dashboards share the same set of template variables. Define them once in a shared library to avoid drift:

// dashboards/lib/variables.libsonnet
local grafana = import 'grafonnet/grafana.libsonnet';
local template = grafana.template;

{
  datasource:: template.datasource(
    name='datasource',
    query='prometheus',
    label='Datasource',
    hide='',
  ),

  feature:: template.new(
    name='feature',
    datasource='$datasource',
    query='label_values(claude_api_request_total, feature)',
    label='Feature',
    refresh='load',
    includeAll=true,
    allValues='.*',
    multi=true,
  ),

  model:: template.new(
    name='model',
    datasource='$datasource',
    query='label_values(claude_api_request_total, model)',
    label='Model',
    refresh='load',
    includeAll=true,
    allValues='.*',
  ),

  interval:: template.interval(
    name='interval',
    label='Interval',
    values=['30s', '1m', '5m', '15m'],
    auto_count=30,
  ),
}
Import these in each dashboard with
local vars = import '../lib/variables.libsonnet';
Template 1: Golden Signals Dashboard
The on-call dashboard. Refresh every 30 seconds. Default time range: last 3 hours. Panels are arranged in a 2-row grid: reliability on row 1, saturation on row 2.
Template 2: Cost Overview Dashboard
The engineering and product dashboard. Refresh every 5 minutes. Default time range: last 7 days. Panels emphasise trends and attribution rather than real-time state.
The token-cost calculations above use hardcoded price constants. Store these in a shared
lib/pricing.libsonnet
file updated whenever Anthropic adjusts pricing, so all dashboards stay in sync.
Template 3: Quality Signals Dashboard
The feature-team dashboard. Refresh every minute. Default time range: last 24 hours. Requires the quality metrics instrumentation from the Quality Signals topic.
Grafana Folder Organisation
Group managed dashboards in a dedicated Grafana folder to make permissions and access control straightforward:
Grafana Folder
Contents
Viewer Access
Claude / Production
golden_signals, cost_overview, quality_signals (managed by CI)
Engineering + product
Claude / Experiments
A/B test dashboards, prompt variant comparisons (editable)
Feature teams
Claude / Archive
Deprecated dashboards kept for reference (read-only)
Engineering
Personal folders
Individual engineers' experimental panels
Dashboard owner only
Create the managed folder once via the Grafana API and note its numeric ID — dashboards deployed by CI should include
"folderId": <id>
in the POST body to land in the right folder automatically.

# Create the managed folder (run once)
curl -sS --fail -X POST "$GRAFANA_URL/api/folders" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "Claude / Production", "uid": "claude-production"}'

# Get the numeric folder ID
FOLDER_ID=$(curl -sS "$GRAFANA_URL/api/folders/claude-production" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" | jq '.id')
echo "Folder ID: $FOLDER_ID"

# Deploy with folder placement
curl -sS --fail -X POST "$GRAFANA_URL/api/dashboards/db" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{
    \"dashboard\": $(cat dashboards/rendered/claude/golden_signals.json),
    \"folderId\": $FOLDER_ID,
    \"overwrite\": true
  }"
Panel Naming and Cross-Dashboard Linking
Consistent panel titles and dashboard links help on-call engineers navigate from a firing alert directly to the relevant panel:
Use sentence case for panel titles:
Request Latency
not
Request_latency
or
LATENCY
Prefix with the metric family when multiple signal types appear in one dashboard:
Eval: Quality Score
vs
Eval: Format Compliance
Add dashboard links: from Golden Signals → link to Cost Overview and Quality Signals dashboards
Include a text panel at the top of each dashboard with a one-sentence description and a link to the relevant runbook
Use threshold lines on time-series panels to show alert thresholds visually — engineers should not need to open the alert YAML to understand what triggers a page
Getting Started Checklist
Use this checklist when deploying the dashboard suite to a new environment:
Clone or vendor grafonnet-lib as a submodule under dashboards/vendor/.
Copy the three template files (
golden_signals.jsonnet
cost_overview.jsonnet
quality_signals.jsonnet
) into dashboards/claude/.
Update any hardcoded model price constants in lib/pricing.libsonnet to match current Anthropic pricing.
Set the GRAFANA_URL and GRAFANA_API_TOKEN environment variables (or repository secrets).
Run `make render-all` to generate the rendered/ JSON files.
Commit rendered/ files alongside the Jsonnet sources.
Create the
claude-production
Grafana folder via the API and note its numeric ID.
Run `make push` to deploy all three dashboards.
Verify each dashboard loads at the expected URL with correct data.
Configure dashboard links between the three dashboards.
Add the CI workflow (.github/workflows/dashboards.yaml) from the previous sub-subtopic.
Summary

The three-dashboard suite — Golden Signals, Cost Overview, Quality Signals — gives every Claude team complete operational visibility from a single, reproducible Jsonnet definition. Each dashboard serves a distinct audience: on-call engineers (reliability), engineering leads (cost), and feature teams (quality). Deploying them via the GitOps workflow ensures they stay in sync with the application code and never silently drift.
Start with Golden Signals; add Cost Overview and Quality Signals as the team instruments those metrics.
Shared lib/ files (variables, pricing, panel builders) keep dashboards consistent and reduce update surface.
Set editable=false on all CI-managed dashboards to prevent UI drift.
Include threshold lines on every time-series so alert thresholds are visible without opening alert YAML.
